source: script/talk-framework-v6.md slide 14 + Algorithmic Justice League research + primary literature
thesis: Stop saying bias. Name what you're seeing.
subThesis: A 5×5 grid of categories that bias laundering hides under. Click any cell to learn what to look for. Mark a cell when you've seen it ship in production. A complete row or column unlocks the framework as a checklist you can take to your next stand-up.

cells.label: Skin tone error rates
cells.look: compare error rates across skin tones; commercial systems often hide a 30+ point gap

cells.label: Gendered authority
cells.look: ask the model for the same role with and without a gender qualifier; watch the authority signals collapse

cells.label: Hiring screens
cells.look: the model didn't invent the discrimination — it learned it; check the training set's own hiring history

cells.label: Predictive policing
cells.look: audits show models direct patrols to neighborhoods where past arrests were concentrated, regardless of present-day crime distribution

cells.label: Search ad targeting
cells.look: Sweeney 2013: searching for African-American-sounding names returned arrest-record ads at much higher rates

cells.label: Risk-assessment scoring
cells.look: ProPublica's COMPAS audit (2016): Black defendants flagged 'high risk' at twice the rate of white defendants for equivalent records

cells.label: AAVE content moderation
cells.look: Sap et al. 2019: hate-speech classifiers flagged AAVE 1.5–2x more often than equivalent Standard American English

cells.label: Disability accommodation
cells.look: captions, alt text, screen-reader output: who is a caption written for, and who is described as 'normal-looking'?

cells.label: Accent + dialect
cells.look: voice assistants miss non-General-American accents at higher rates; check word-error-rate by demographic

cells.label: Body size + weight
cells.look: image models default 'fit'/'professional' to thin; see how the model fills the gap when size is not specified

cells.label: Age
cells.look: image generators erase wrinkles, gray hair, and laugh lines unless asked; ask for an age and watch the prompt fight you

cells.label: the room you're in
cells.look: the bingo center. Whoever's missing from the room you build is the bias you ship.

cells.label: Trans + nonbinary erasure
cells.look: models default to binary genders; pronouns collapse to he/she even when they/them is in the prompt

cells.label: Indigenous representation
cells.look: search 'Indigenous person' and audit for present-tense, contemporary clothing, urban settings, plurality of nations

cells.label: Religious depiction
cells.look: ask for a 'religious leader' five times; count which traditions appear and which never do

cells.label: Caste
cells.look: models trained on South Asian web data carry caste hierarchies; watch how 'professional' vs 'worker' images differ in skin tone, dress, surname

cells.label: Class + wealth signals
cells.look: models read 'wealthy' as light skin, fitted clothing, glass architecture; 'poor' as the opposite. Audit the visual shorthand.

cells.label: Mental health framing
cells.look: search 'depressed' or 'addicted'; audit for criminalizing imagery vs. care imagery

cells.label: Names → race
cells.look: swap 'DeShawn' for 'Brad' in the same resume prompt; audit recommended salary, role, character traits

cells.label: Refugee + asylum framing
cells.look: headlines auto-generated for refugees vs. expats from the same country: count the qualifying adjectives

cells.label: Pregnancy + caregiving
cells.look: ask for 'a CEO who took parental leave'; watch the model rewrite the role

cells.label: Criminal background
cells.look: background-check models retroactively punish people for arrests that didn't lead to convictions; the model launders this as 'risk'

cells.label: Education credentialism
cells.look: image of 'a smart person' vs. 'a graduate'; audit which schools and which body types appear

cells.label: Region + rurality
cells.look: ask for an 'expert' located in a small town; watch the model relocate them to a major city

cells.label: Native language assumptions
cells.look: the model assumes English-default; non-English prompts are translated, summarized, and flattened. Watch what's lost.
